title: Worktree path joined with raw branch name allows path traversal via slashes
description:
## Severity
Critical

## Dimension
Security and Access / Input Handling

## Location
src/tools/worktree.rs:84 (worktree_dir construction) and 60-67 (branch arg extraction)

## Violation
```rust
let branch = args.get("branch").and_then(|v| v.as_str()).unwrap_or("").to_string();
if branch.is_empty() { return ("Error: branch name is required", true); }
...
let worktree_dir = git_root.join(".worktrees").join(&branch);
```

The branch name is taken verbatim from model input and `.join()`-ed into the worktree path with no validation. A model (or a prompt injector) supplying `branch: "../../tmp/escape"` causes `git_root.join(".worktrees").join("../../tmp/escape")` to resolve to `/tmp/escape` — completely outside the worktrees directory and outside the project root jail. The strict-mode resolve_path used by file tools is NOT applied here because the worktree path is constructed inside the tool itself.

Git itself ALSO accepts branch names like `..`, slashes, and unicode — `git worktree add /tmp/escape ..` will succeed. From the same input the tool then calls `set_current_dir(&worktree_dir)` — landing the process anywhere on disk.

## Mandated refactor
1. Validate branch name with a strict allowlist regex: `^[a-zA-Z0-9][a-zA-Z0-9._/-]{0,127}$` AND reject `..`, `//`, leading `/`, leading `-`, and trailing `.lock`. Mirror git's `check-ref-format --branch` (or shell out to `git check-ref-format`).
2. After computing worktree_dir, run resolve_path-style containment: assert `worktree_dir.starts_with(git_root.join(".worktrees"))`. Refuse if not.

## Filed from QA review dispatch
